title: smoke-hot French potatoes tartiflette
description: A fatty, pillowy, comforting French dish rich with flavors of green cardamom, smoked paprika, and tender potatoes, perfect for a cold winter evening. This pescatarian-friendly recipe makes a satisfying dinner for the family.
instructions: Peel and thinly slice the potatoes.Boil the sliced potatoes in a large pot of salted water for 5 minutes.
Drain the potatoes and set them aside.In a large skillet, melt 2 tablespoons of butter over medium heat.
Add the sliced onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute more.
Add the smoked paprika and stir for 1 minute.
In a large bowl, whisk together the flour and gruyère cheese.
Add the heavy cream to the bowl and whisk until smooth.
In a greased 9x13-inch baking dish, spread a layer of the potato slices.
Pour some of the cream mixture over the potatoes, followed by a sprinkle of the gruyère mixture.Repeat this process until all the ingredients are used up.
Dot the top of the tartiflette with the remaining 2 tablespoons of butter.
Bake the tartiflette in the preheated oven for 35-40 minutes, or until the top is golden brown and the potatoes are tender.
